debug_info.goal:
case neg.inl.fstE
n : ℕ
S : Error.Dist n
a b : Error.Agent n
σ : List (Error.Call n)
this : @S b = true
err✝ callee✝ : Error.Agent n
o : Error.maxOne n (Error.Call.fstE n a err✝ callee✝ :: σ)
a_not_know_before_t :
  ¬Error.eval n S ⟨σ, Error.Sequence.maxOne_cons n (Error.Call.fstE n a err✝ callee✝) σ o⟩
      (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true)))
a_not_know_before_f :
  ¬Error.eval n S ⟨σ, Error.Sequence.maxOne_cons n (Error.Call.fstE n a err✝ callee✝) σ o⟩
      (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, false)))
a_knows_after_t :
  Error.eval n S ⟨Error.Call.fstE n a err✝ callee✝ :: σ, o⟩ (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true)))
⊢ failed to pretty print expression (use 'set_option pp.rawOnError true' for raw representation)

failed_attempts:
simp_all [stubbornness]
            split_ifs <;> simp_all
            grind

· -- (b, ! S b) ∉ ...
               -- Not in partner (stubbornness).
               -- Not in self (otherwise conflict -> not K).
               -- Since we can't easily prove "conflict -> not K" here without a lemma,
               -- we rely on the `delete` mechanism for direct calls, and `grind` for logic.
               grind
p.

try cases C
            cases S b <;> simp_all

all_goals sorry
    case Callee => all_goals sorry
    case Other =>
      simp only [roleOfIn_eq_Other_iff] at ra
      rw [not_in_call_then_invariant_kv ra] at a_kv_b
      rw [not_in_call_then_invariant_resultSet ra]
      exact IH (⁻o) a_kv_b

simp_all; try { refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩; use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; simp_all; use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use ⌜_⌝; simp; intro; simp_all; apply equiv_then_know_same at a_knows_after_f; assumption; exact a_knows_after_f; simp; simp }
             by_cases be : b = e
             · subst be; exfalso
               have : ¬ eval S ⟨Error.Call.sndE a _ b :: σ, o⟩ (K a (Neg (b@b))) := by
                 simp [eval]; use (S.switch b), ⟨Error.Call.normal a _ :: σ, by simp [maxOne]⟩
                 constructor; swap
                 · unfold equiv; simp [roleOfIn, contribSet, Dist.switch]; simp_all
                 · simp [stubbornness, Dist.switch]
               contradiction
             · simp_all; refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
               · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp
               · simp_all
               · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use ⌜_⌝; simp
               · intro; simp_all; apply equiv_then_know_same at a_knows_after_f; assumption; exact a_knows_after_f; simp; simp
    case Callee =>
      by_cases h : eval S ⟨σ,⁻o⟩ (Kv a b)
      · specialize IH (⁻o) h
        unfold resultSet
        simp only [ra, stubbornness, Subtype.forall, OSequence.length_def]
        let C_copy := C
        cases C <;> simp_all <;> subst ra
        all_goals
          refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
          · simp [eval, stubbornness, Subtype.forall, OSequence.length_def, not_forall, Bool.not_eq_not]
            use S; simp only [and_true]; use ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp
          · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use C_copy; unfold C_copy; simp [roleOfIn]
          · rw [eval_dis] at h
            intro n1 n2 T τ len equ
            cases h_b : S b <;> exfalso <;> simp_all only [Bool.not_false, Bool.not_true, or_false, false_or]
            have := true_of_knowldege h; simp_all
      · clear IH
        rw [eval_dis] at h; push_neg at h
        rcases h with ⟨a_not_know_before_t, a_not_know_before_f⟩
        rw [eval_dis] at a_kv_b
        rcases a_kv_b with (a_knows_after_t | a_knows_after_f)
        · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
          simp at this
          unfold resultSet
          cases C <;> simp [ra] <;> simp at ra <;> subst ra
          all_goals
             simp_all; try { refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩; use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; simp_all; use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use ⌜_⌝; simp; intro; simp_all; apply equiv_then_know_same at a_knows_after_t; assumption; exact a_knows_after_t; simp; simp }
             by_cases be : b = e
             · subst be; exfalso
               have : ¬ eval S ⟨Error.Call.fstE _ _ b :: σ, o⟩ (K a (b@b)) := by
                 simp [eval]; use (S.switch b), ⟨Error.Call.normal _ a :: σ, by simp [maxOne]⟩
                 constructor; swap
                 · unfold equiv; simp [roleOfIn, contribSet, Dist.switch]; simp_all
                 · simp [stubbornness, Dist.switch]
               contradiction
             · simp_all; refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
               · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp
               · simp_all
               · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use ⌜_⌝; simp
               · intro; simp_all; apply equiv_then_know_same at a_knows_after_t; assumption; exact a_knows_after_t; simp; simp
        · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_f
          simp at this
          unfold resultSet
          cases C <;> simp [ra] <;> simp at ra <;> subst ra
          all_goals
             simp_all; try { refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩; use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; simp_all; use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use ⌜_⌝; simp; intro; simp_all; apply equiv_then_know_same at a_knows_after_f; assumption; exact a_knows_after_f; simp; simp }
             by_cases be : b = e
             · subst be; exfalso
               have : ¬ eval S ⟨Error.Call.fstE _ _ b :: σ, o⟩ (K a (Neg (b@b))) := by
                 simp [eval]; use (S.switch b), ⟨Error.Call.normal _ a :: σ, by simp [maxOne]⟩
                 constructor; swap
                 · unfold equiv; simp [roleOfIn, contribSet, Dist.switch]; simp_all
                 · simp [stubbornness, Dist.switch]
               contradiction
             · simp_all; refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
               · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp
               · simp_all
               · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use ⌜_⌝; simp
               · intro; simp_all; apply equiv_then_know_same at a_knows_after_f; assumption; exact a_knows_after_f; simp; simp
    case Other =>
      rw [not_in_call_then_invariant_resultSet ra, not_in_call_then_invariant_kv ra] at *
      exact IH (⁻o) a_kv_b
· apply h_false; exact ⟨he, hr, hc⟩
sorry

simp [resultSet, roleOfIn, true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t] at *
            refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
            · exact true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
            · simp only [Set.mem_diff, not_and, not_not, Set.mem_union, not_or, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
              rintro ((_ | _) | _) h_not_del
              all_goals
                -- In fstE/sndE, σ must be errFree (due to maxOne).
                -- Since S b = true, only (b, true) propagates. (b, false) is impossible.
                -- For sndE with error on b, we derive contradiction with knowledge.
                exfalso
                have : errFree σ := by
                  cases ‹Call› <;> simp_all [maxOne]
                have not_false_in_res : (b, false) ∉ resultSet a S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ ∧ (b, false) ∉ resultSet b S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ := by
                   -- Since σ is errFree and S b = true, no agent has (b, false).
                   -- This requires a small lemma or induction, but here we can try stubborness
                   -- or just use the fact that errors are needed to create false.
                   -- Given "short/simple", we rely on the fact that false simply doesn't exist.
                   -- But strictly, we need to prove it.
                   -- Alternatively, use the 'delete' argument:
                   apply h_not_del
                   intros T τ D equ _ _
                   -- a knows b=true, so T b must be true.
                   -- a_knows_after_t ensures eval T (Has b (b, true)).
                   -- Stubbornness ensures T b = true.
                   -- So eval T (Has b (b, !false)) holds.
                   apply a_knows_after_t T ⟨D :: τ.1, Sequence.maxOne_cons (Sequence.maxOne_of_errFree τ.2)⟩
                   · simp [OSequence.length]; exact equ.1
                   · simp [equiv, OSequence.length]; exact equ

simp_all
            constructor
            · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t; simp_all [eval]
            · intro h; simp [resultSet] at h; rcases h with ⟨_, not_del⟩
              apply not_del; intro T τ D equ _ _; exact a_knows_after_t T τ equ
        · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_f
          simp at this
          cases C <;> simp [ra] <;> simp at ra <;> subst ra <;> simp_all
          all_goals
            constructor
            · assumption
            · intro h; simp [resultSet] at h; rcases h with ⟨_, not_del⟩
              apply not_del; intro T τ D equ _ _; exact a_knows_after_f T τ equ
    case Callee =>
      -- "... we distinguish two cases."
      by_cases h : eval S ⟨σ,⁻o⟩ (Kv a b)
      · -- "Either already ...""
        specialize IH (⁻o) h
        unfold resultSet
        simp only [ra, stubbornness, Subtype.forall, OSequence.length_def]
        let C_copy := C
        cases C <;> simp_all <;> subst ra
        all_goals
          refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
          · simp [eval, stubbornness, Subtype.forall, OSequence.length_def, not_forall,
              Bool.not_eq_not]
            use S
            simp only [and_true]
            use ⟨σ,⁻o⟩
            simp
          · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩; simp; use C_copy; unfold C_copy; simp [roleOfIn]
          · rw [eval_dis] at h
            intro not_in_caller not_a_knows T τ same_len equ
            cases h_b : S b
            all_goals
              exfalso
              simp_all only [Bool.not_false, Bool.not_true, or_false, false_or]
              have := true_of_knowldege h
              simp_all
      · -- "Or ..."
        clear IH
        rw [eval_dis] at h
        push_neg at h
        rcases h with ⟨a_not_know_before_t, a_not_know_before_f⟩
        rw [eval_dis] at a_kv_b
        rcases a_kv_b with (a_knows_after_t | a_knows_after_f)
        · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
          simp at this
          cases C <;> simp [ra] <;> simp at ra <;> subst ra <;> simp_all
          all_goals
            constructor
            · assumption
            · intro h; simp [resultSet] at h; rcases h with ⟨_, not_del⟩
              apply not_del; intro T τ D equ _ _; exact a_knows_after_t T τ equ
        · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_f
          simp at this
          cases C <;> simp [ra] <;> simp at ra <;> subst ra <;> simp_all
          all_goals
            constructor
            · assumption
            · intro h; simp [resultSet] at h; rcases h with ⟨_, not_del⟩
              apply not_del; intro T τ D equ _ _; exact a_knows_after_f T τ equ
    case Other =>
      simp_all [not_in_call_then_invariant_resultSet, not_in_call_then_invariant_kv]

-- Cases: fstE and sndE (where a is Caller)
            cases S b <;> simp_all
            constructor
            · apply true_of_knowldege at a_knows_after_t
              assumption
            · -- We must show (b, !S b) is not in the set.
              -- Since (b, S b) is in the set (proven above) and a knows it,
              -- the incorrect value (b, !S b) must be deleted by the `delete` mechanism.
              -- This is guaranteed because `a` has knowledge `K a (b, S b)`.
              simp [resultSet]
              intro h_in
              have in_del : (b, !S b) ∈ delete Prod.snd := by
                simp [resultSet]
                intros T τ D heq hrole hsel
                -- We apply the knowledge a_knows_after_t to the world (T, D::τ).
                -- Note: Rigorously, we would need to show (S, C::σ) ~ (T, D::τ).
                -- The delete condition ensures everything matches except potentially C.pair = D.pair.
                -- However, for the purpose of this lemma, we rely on the fact that
                -- if the message content `sel` drives the knowledge, it holds.
                -- Given the constraints, we assume this holds or use available tactics.
                have K_holds := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
                simp_all
              -- Contradiction: h_in says it's in the set (so NOT in delete),
              -- but we showed it IS in delete.
              have not_in_del := (Set.mem_diff _).mp h_in |>.right
              contradiction

case fstE =>
                -- Symmetric to normal/fstE: message is (b, true). (b, false) not added.
                simp [resultSet] at h_false
                rcases h_false with ⟨_, h_del⟩ | ⟨_, h_del⟩ <;>
                { apply h_del; intro T τ D heq hrole hsel; sorry }

cases S b <;> simp_all
            · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t; simp_all
            · refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
              · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t; simp_all
              · intro h; rcases h with ⟨_, h⟩; apply h; simp
                intros T τ D _ _ _
                apply a_knows_after_t
                simp_all [equiv]

at this
          unfold resultSet
          cases C <;> simp [ra] <;> simp at ra <;> subst ra
          all_goals sorry
    case Callee => sorry
    case Other => sorry

cases h_sb : S b <;> simp_all
            rename_i err_agent
            by_cases err_on_b : (err_agent = b)
            · -- Error on b. a sees true (inverted false).
              -- a knows b is false.
              -- Construct T where b=true (switch S). D = normal.
              -- a sees true in T.
              -- Equiv holds. a must know b=false in T. Impossible.
              exfalso; sorry
            · -- Error not on b.
              refine ⟨⟨Or.inr ?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
              · simp [invert]; use b, false; simp [err_on_b]
              · simp [invert]; intro k h; simp [err_on_b] at h; simp at h
              · simp
    case Callee =>
      -- Symmetric to Caller
      sorry
    case Other =>
      simp [ra] at a_kv_b
      rw [not_in_call_then_invariant_kv ra] at a_kv_b
      exact IH (⁻o) a_kv_b

· sorry
    all_goals sorry

· right; simp [equiv]; refine ⟨by simp, ?_, ?_⟩
                    · simp [roleOfIn]
                    · simp [contribSet, roleOfIn, T, Dist.switch]; congr; ext; simp [Dist.switch]; split <;> simp_all
               rcases equiv_T with eq|eq <;>
               have k_false := a_knows_after T ⟨Call.normal a b :: σ, by aesop⟩ (by simp [OSequence.length]; exact eq.1) eq <;>
               simp [eval, stubbornness, T, Dist.switch] at k_false <;>
               simp_all
          · intro; contradiction -- trivially true for union

rcases a_kv_b with (a_knows_after_t | a_knows_after_f)
        · have := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
          simp at this
          unfold resultSet
          cases C <;> simp [ra] <;> simp at ra <;> subst ra
          case neg.inl.normal c =>
            refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
            · -- ???
              -- Why must `a` already have one of the values b or not-b after σ?
              sorry
            · cases S b <;>  simp_all
            · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩
              simp
              expose_names -- pfoei
              use ⌜a c⌝
              simp
            · intro not_in_callee not_a_knows T τ same_len equ C role_C same_contrib
              cases h_b : S b
              all_goals
                simp_all
              -- ???
              -- How do we know that `S b = T b` here?
              sorry
          all_goals
            sorry
        · sorry
    case Callee =>
      sorry
    case Other =>
      simp [not_in_call_then_invariant_resultSet ra]
      simp [not_in_call_then_invariant_kv ra] at a_kv_b
      exact IH (⁻o) a_kv_b

refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
             · right; simp [c_has] -- It came from c
             · intro; contradiction -- Not refused
             · intro T τ D eq _ _ -- Not deleted
               rcases a_kv_b with h|h
               all_goals
                  cases (S b) <;> simp_all
                  apply equiv_then_know_same ⟨by simp; exact eq.1⟩ at h
                  have := true_of_knowldege (h T ⟨_, eq⟩)
                  simp [stubbornness] at this ⊢
                  assumption

      exact ⟨has_right, not_wrong⟩

constructor
            · exact true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
            · intro h_in
              simp [resultSet] at h_in
              rcases h_in with ⟨_, h_not_del⟩
              apply h_not_del
              -- We prove (b, false) is in the delete set, i.e., a knows b is true.
              -- The delete condition matches the knowledge condition.
              intro T τ D eq_prev role_eq obs_eq
              have eq_total : equiv a (S, ⟨_ :: σ, o⟩) (T, ⟨D :: τ, ⁻(by grind)⟩) := by
                simp [equiv, role_eq, obs_eq]
                use eq_prev
              have k := a_knows_after_t T ⟨D::τ, ⁻(by grind)⟩ (by simp [OSequence.length]; exact eq_prev.1) eq_total
              rw [stubbornness _ _ rfl] at k ⊢
              exact k

-- Cases fstE and sndE.
            -- Since the call has an error, the history σ must be error-free (by maxOne).
            -- This simplifies the situation as agents hold correct values.
            simp_all
            refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
            · -- Proof that a receives correct value (follows from errFree)
              sorry
            · -- Proof that a does not receive incorrect value
              sorry

refine ⟨⟨⟨sorry, sorry⟩, ?_⟩, sorry⟩
            use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩
            simp; use ⌜a c⌝; simp
          case neg.inl.sndE c _ _ =>
            refine ⟨⟨⟨sorry, sorry⟩, ?_⟩, sorry⟩
            use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩
            simp; use ⌜a c⌝; simp

simp_all
              sorry
          case neg.inl.sndE callee err =>
            refine ⟨⟨⟨sorry, sorry⟩, sorry⟩, sorry⟩
            · cases S b <;> simp_all
            · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩
              simp
              use ⌜a callee^err⌝
              simp
            · intro not_in_callee not_a_knows T τ same_len equ C role_C same_contrib
              cases h_b : S b
              all_goals
                simp_all
              sorry

constructor
            · apply true_of_knowldege; assumption
            · intro h
              simp [resultSet, roleOfIn, invert] at h
              rcases h with h | h
              · exact a_not_know_before_f ((local_is_known false).1 S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ h)
              · if heq : (match C with | ⌜ _ _^e ⌝ => e = b | _ => false) then
                  simp [heq, invert] at h
                  have kt := true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
                  simp [resultSet, roleOfIn, invert, heq] at kt
                  rcases kt with kt | kt
                  · exact a_not_know_before_t ((local_is_known true).1 S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ kt)
                  · have := true_of_knowldege ((local_is_known false).1 S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ kt)
                    simp [eval, this] at this
                else
                  simp [invert, heq] at h
                  have := true_of_knowldege ((local_is_known false).1 S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ h)
                  simp [eval, this] at this

simp only [resultSet, roleOfIn_fstE_eq_Caller_iff, roleOfIn_sndE_eq_Caller_iff]
            constructor
            · exact true_of_knowldege a_knows_after_t
            · intro h
              rcases h with ⟨_, h_del⟩
              apply h_del
              intro T τ D he _ _
              apply a_knows_after_t T τ he

simp_all
              sorry
          case neg.inl.sndE c e =>
            refine ⟨⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
            · sorry
            · cases S b <;> simp_all
            · use S, ⟨σ,⁻o⟩
              simp
              expose_names
              use ⌜a c^e⌝
              simp
            · intro not_in_callee not_a_knows T τ same_len equ C role_C same_contrib
              cases h_b : S b
              all_goals
                simp_all
              sorry
